howItWorks.intro: This batting average calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Walks, sacrifices, and hit-by-pitch are NOT counted as at-bats
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: What is a good batting average?
faq.answer: In MLB, .300+ is excellent, .270-.299 is good, .250-.269 is average, and below .230 is poor. The overall MLB average hovers around .250. In Little League and high school, averages tend to be higher.

faq.question: What counts as an at-bat?
faq.answer: An at-bat is any plate appearance that results in a hit, out, error, or fielder's choice. Walks, hit-by-pitches, sacrifices, and catcher's interference are NOT counted as at-bats.

faq.question: Has anyone batted .400 in MLB?
faq.answer: Ted Williams was the last player to hit .400, batting .406 in 1941. No player has hit .400 since, making it one of baseball's most elusive achievements.

faq.question: Is the Batting Average Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard sports analytics formulas trusted by athletes, coaches, and fans. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Batting Average Calculator?
sections.body: The Batting Average Calculator is a free online tool designed for athletes, coaches, and fans who need quick, accurate calculations in the sports analytics space. By entering your hits, at bats, you get instant results including batting average, rating, hits for .300. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting batting average right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In sports analytics, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by athletes, coaches, and fans worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to analyze performance with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your hits and need to find the right batting average. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Hits
howToSteps.description: Type or select your hits in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.description: Type or select your at bats in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your batting average, rating, hits for .300. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.
